lessons.name: Welcome to Artemis Support Program
lessons.description: Get oriented with the mission control interface and verify the rover connection.
lessons.template:
from lineRobot import Robot

print("ARTEMIS LTV SYSTEM DIAGNOSTIC")
print("Initializing hardware...")

# Initialize LTV unit
ltv_unit = Robot()

print("[TEST 1] Checking Main Drive...")
ltv_unit.move_forward_distance(15)
ltv_unit.move_backward_distance(15)

print("[TEST 2] Checking Steering Mechanism...")
ltv_unit.turn_left()
ltv_unit.turn_right()

print("DIAGNOSTIC COMPLETE. SYSTEMS NOMINAL.")
print("Welcome to the team!")

lessons.name: Concept of Error
lessons.description: Build a diagnostic sweep function to read and print line position Error values using track_line().
lessons.template:
import machine
import time
from lineRobot import Robot
from octoliner import Octoliner

# Pre-written Diagnostic Protocol
def diagnostic_sweep(speed_left, speed_right):
    print("Starting sweep...")
    for i in range(5):
        robot.run_motors_speed(speed_left, speed_right)
        time.sleep(0.1)
        robot.stop()
        
        # TODO: Read the line position using track_line() and print the Error variable to the terminal
        
        time.sleep(0.4)
        
    print("Returning to center...")
    robot.run_motors_speed(-speed_left, -speed_right)
    time.sleep(0.4)
    robot.stop()
    print("Sweep complete.")

# Setup
robot = Robot()
i2c = machine.I2C(scl=machine.Pin(22), sda=machine.Pin(21), freq=100000)
octoliner = Octoliner()
octoliner.begin(i2c)
octoliner.set_sensitivity(240)

# Mission Sequence (Call the function, move, call again)

name: Debugging & Code Quality
description: Master debugging techniques, exception handling, and code refactoring.

lessons.name: The Art of Debugging
lessons.description: Find and fix 6 bugs in broken P-controller code using systematic debugging techniques.
lessons.template:
import time
from lineRobot import Robot

robot = Robot()
octoliner = robot.octoliner

base_speed = 40
kp = 15

while True  # Bug 1: Missing colon
    sensor_array = octoliner.analog_read_all()
    print(sensor)  # Bug 2: NameError - should be sensor_array
    
    if max(sensor_array) > 700:  # Bug 3: Wrong operator - should be <
        robot.stop()
        print("End of line detected!")
        break
    
    position = octoliner.track_line()
    P = kp + position  # Bug 4: Wrong operator - should be *
    
    left_speed = base_speed + P   # Bug 5: Wrong sign - should be -
    right_speed = base_speed + P
    
    robot.run_motor_speed(left_speed, right_speed)  # Bug 6: Typo - should be run_motors_speed
    time.sleep(0.01)

lessons.name: The Hardware Safety Net
lessons.description: Implement try-except blocks to handle sensor errors gracefully without crashing.
lessons.template:
import time
from lineRobot import Robot
robot = Robot()

def detect_color_name(r, g, b):
    total = r + g + b

    # TODO: Protect the math below with try-except ZeroDivisionError
    # If an error occurs, print a warning and return "Unknown"

    r_ratio = r / total
    g_ratio = g / total

    if r_ratio > 0.4:
        return "Red"
    elif g_ratio > 0.4:
        return "Green"
    else:
        return "Floor"

# Simulated scans with injected "Shadow Zones" (0,0,0)
scans = [(140, 30, 30), (0, 0, 0), (50, 150, 50), (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 0), 
         (60, 60, 60), (150, 40, 40), (0, 0, 0), (40, 140, 40), (70, 70, 70)]

scan_num = 1
for r, g, b in scans:
    robot.run_motors_speed(25, -25)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    robot.stop()

    print(f"Sector #{scan_num} | RGB: ({r},{g},{b})")

    # The program will crash here on the first (0,0,0) glitch!
    color = detect_color_name(r, g, b)
    time.sleep(0.1)
    print(f"ANALYSIS: {color}\n")
    scan_num += 1

print("Survey Complete")

lessons.name: Code Clinic (Refactoring)
lessons.description: Refactor spaghetti code into clean, modular functions with proper naming conventions.
lessons.template:
import time
from lineRobot import Robot

robot = Robot()
t = robot.tcs3472
o = robot.octoliner

# TODO: Replace these cryptic variable names with descriptive UPPERCASE constants
s = 240
lim = 700
v = 30
k = 12

# TODO: Create three functions:
# 1. get_mineral_color() - with try-except for ZeroDivisionError
# 2. calculate_steering() - returns correction value
# 3. apply_movement(correction) - controls motors

while True:
    a = o.analog_read_all()
    
    if max(a) < lim:
        robot.stop()
        print("End")
        break
    
    t.integration_time(s)
    r, g, b = t.rgb()
    tot = r + g + b
    
    if tot > 0:
        if r / tot > 0.5:
            print("Red mineral!")
        elif g / tot > 0.5:
            print("Green mineral!")
    
    pos = o.track_line()
    c = k * pos
    l = v - c
    rt = v + c
    robot.run_motors_speed(l, rt)
    time.sleep(0.01)
